codeReview.summary: The landed commit correctly hardens the discovery webhook so an unset webhook secret now fails closed and it sanitizes several discovery-test literals, but the shipped feature is incomplete relative to its own description. The only successful feature commit touches discovery-worker files, while the promised OAuth-session and local-service boundary work remains outside the landed diff, and one touched webhook regression test no longer exercises the missing-sheetId path its name claims to cover.

issues.description: The feature description explicitly covers OAuth session handling and commit-clean regression tests, but the only successful feature commit (`git show --name-only ac86145`) does not include `app.js`, `server/index.mjs`, `server/security-boundaries.mjs`, or the new root regression tests. Those tests are still untracked (`git status --short` shows `tests/oauth-session-storage-boundary.test.mjs` and `tests/server-security-boundaries.test.mjs`), and this untracked OAuth test still uses `secret_token_123`, so the promised hardening and placeholder-literal cleanup were not actually landed.

This regression test no longer verifies the behavior named in its title. It is titled `accepts missing request sheetId when worker config provides one`, but the request body constructed at lines 238-243 includes `sheetId: "sheet_123"`. That leaves the local-mode sheetId fallback path without the regression coverage the test name suggests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The successful worker session did not follow the integration-refactor skill's required context-reading step, which makes it easier to miss contract or environment requirements during security work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `integration-refactor-worker/SKILL.md` step 1 requires reading `.factory/library/contracts.md` and `.factory/library/environment.md`, but the `e48c8be0-8693-4046-9e4b-83f4836ab69c` transcript skeleton only shows reads for `mission.md`, `validation-contract.md`, `features.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `.factory/services.yaml`, and `.factory/library/user-testing.md` before edits.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The canonical discovery-worker service manifest does not surface the new webhook-auth requirement, so validators that rely on `.factory/services.yaml` alone will start a worker whose POST routes 401 unless they separately discover the header requirement from another doc.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/services.yaml` starts `discovery_worker` without any `BROWSER_USE_DISCOVERY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`/`DISCOVERY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` env, while `.factory/library/user-testing.md` notes that the worker now requires `x-discovery-secret` on POST routes and browser POSTs to `http://127.0.0.1:8644` are expected to receive 401.

summary: Fail: the landed commit fixes the discovery webhook's fail-open secret check, but the broader OAuth/session and local-service hardening described for this feature was not actually landed, and one touched webhook regression test no longer covers the missing-sheetId case it claims to protect.
